stimulus:
The following text is from Elena Carrasco's 1888 poem “To My Detractors.”

THEY murmur that I scorn the customs of my fathers;

But in truth I neither honor nor despise such customs

(For what have I to do with them?—or what with their unmaking?),

Only I will plant in every plaza of these mountain towns, on the high pastures and along the rivers,

And in the markets, and above every cart laden with grain that creaks the road,

Without altars, or codes, or wardens, or any decree,

The custom of free fellowship among the people.
stem: Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?

answerOptions.content: The speaker responds to a charge brought against him, then declares an expansive aim of his own.
The speaker laments a loss of community, then forecasts a sweeping political reform.
The speaker doubts a fashionable opinion, then summarizes the convictions he has long held.
The speaker admits his own faults, then catalogs the honors he has received.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The poem opens by noting a charge against the speaker (that he scorns the customs of his fathers). The speaker then addresses that charge (claiming he neither honors nor despises such customs) and concludes by announcing a sweeping aim: to plant “the custom of free fellowship” throughout the country. The overall structure is therefore an address to a criticism followed by the declaration of an expansive ambition.

Choice B is incorrect because the speaker does not lament a loss of community or predict a specific political reform; the speaker states his own goal rather than predicting events. Choice C is incorrect because the speaker addresses an accusation he considers untrue rather than questioning a prevalent opinion, and he ends by stating a future aim, not a long-held worldview. Choice D is incorrect because the speaker does not concede any personal faults and does not list any achievements.
